item.DESCRIPT: Mapping for Line Based Approval Settings
item.AUTHOR: PSABINO

item.DESCRIPT: Determine AFS ID for Level Based Approval
item.AUTHOR: PSABINO

item.DESCRIPT: Next Method for Header Level Based Approval
item.AUTHOR: PSABINO

item.DESCRIPT: Next Method for Parallel Line Based Approval
item.AUTHOR: PSABINO

item.DESCRIPT: Mapping for Line Based Approval Settings
item.AUTHOR: PSABINO

item.DESCRIPT: Open Text User Mapping Object ID
item.AUTHOR: PSABINO

item.DESCRIPT: Open Text User Mapping Object ID
item.AUTHOR: PSABINO

item.DESCRIPT: Open Text User Mapping Object ID
item.AUTHOR: PSABINO

item.DESCRIPT: Open Text User Mapping Object ID
item.AUTHOR: PSABINO

item.DESCRIPT: Open Text User Mapping Object ID
item.AUTHOR: PSABINO

item.DESCRIPT: Open Text User Mapping Object ID
item.AUTHOR: PSABINO

item.DESCRIPT: LBA is not configured for this DocID
item.AUTHOR: PSABINO

LOCALS_DEF.item: *"* use this source file for any type declarations (class
*"* definitions, interfaces or data types) you need for method

LOCALS_IMP.item: *"* local class implementation for public class
*"* use this source file for the implementation part of

LOCALS_MAC.item: *"* use this source file for any macro definitions you need
*"* in the implementation part of the class

DETERMINE_LBA.item: IF ( ( iv_device = lv_device1 OR iv_device = lv_device2 OR iv_device = lv_device3 ) AND ( iv_1head IS NOT INITIAL ) ).
IF ls_1head-doctype IS NOT INITIAL. "INSERT VIMI-25673:ASUTHARA
*-- Get Global AFS Setting for all remaining Doctypes (Empty Doctype)
*-- Get AFS ID from AFS Setting

GET_OBJECT_KEY.item: DATA: lt_wiobj TYPE TABLE OF sww_wi2obj.
*-- Get Workitem ID if not provided
*-- Get Object Type and Object Key for Workitem ID
READ TABLE lt_wiobj INTO ls_wiobj WITH KEY typeid = '/ORS/INVAP'.
READ TABLE lt_wiobj INTO ls_wiobj WITH KEY typeid = '/OPT/V1001'.
IF lv_invtype = 'NPO' AND lv_bus_objtype IS INITIAL.
ELSEIF lv_invtype = 'LIV' AND lv_bus_objtype IS INITIAL.

NEXT_HEADER.item: *-- Read open Approval History for the delivered Level
*-- Get Open Items for the level
LOOP AT lt_appr_hist INTO ls_appr_hist WHERE status = 'N'. "New, not assigned yet
*-- All Lines in current Level have been processed => check for next Level
LOOP AT lt_appr_hist INTO ls_appr_hist WHERE status = 'Y' "Currently Approved
IF lt_open_lines IS INITIAL. "Approver has done the final approve
*-- Get Approver List for the next Level

NEXT_LINE.item: *-- Read open Approval History for the delivered Level
* Build a list of currently assigned approvers for open lines check
*-- Get Open Items for the level (Status Z (currently final approved will be updated after approval to X)
WHERE status = 'N' OR "New, not assigned yet
( status CA 'YZ' AND current_approver <> actual_approver ). "or touched by someone else "VIMI-9863
*-- For parallel we need not do determine next approvers (Coder/Requesters runs anyway sequential)
* In case there are no approvers for the line item,
* add it into the list of open ones for the current level
* if none of current approvers can approve, it is open
* the check will be done inside of lba_get_approver_list_line method
*-- Just one line is needed for Sequential Approval and also for Coding/Requester Step
*-- All Lines in current Level have been processed => check for next Level
LOOP AT lt_appr_hist INTO ls_appr_hist WHERE status = 'Y' "Currently Approved
( lv_new_par IS INITIAL AND "For parallel we need to determine next approvers only in last approval
* the check will be done inside of lba_get_approver_list_line method
*-- Just one line is needed for Sequential Approval and also for Coding/Requester Step
* When substitutes inherit the COA, then when searching the next approver
* we need to exclude the originally assigned approver from the result.
* When no inheritance, we exclude the user who is executing the workitem.
*-- Get Approver List for the relevant Lines

INIT.item: * MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO

NEW.item: * Method to find the initial User (Coder/Requester)
*-- Get Usermap Class either by Configuration or Z_CONST
*-- Get Coder by Customizing (FM, Requester, BUKRS, ...)
*-- Set Current Level to 1 as Coding is done by Requester / Path is always 1 at start
** CR3 - Requester step should be skipped - Start
* current_path_level-appr_level = 2. "CR3, Requester step should be skipped
* "Now we should provide LT_APPROVER table for first level of approval
* "Now let's extract the approvers for this level from ARCOA
* SELECT comp_code, appr_level, opt_userid FROM /opt/appr_coa INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_appr_coa)
** CR3 - Requester step should be skipped - End
*-- Set Current Level to 0 (Coder)/ Path is always 1 at start
*-- sort to make default coder as last one
*-- all found coder are not valid
*-- Get the Approval Flags for the Coder (Even if Requester = Coder)
IF sy-subrc = 0 AND NOT ls_flags IS INITIAL.
ls_approver-look_ahead = space. "Look ahead is not available in LBA
IF ms_afs-afs_type = 'P' AND current_path_level-appr_level >= 1. "VIMI-19327 - only for requesters and approvers
ls_approver-override = space. "No overwrite for parallel in LBA

NEXT.item: *-- Distinguish between Header and Line Based Approval
* Search method that returns all appr
* If constant is set and sequential approval and approval not completed
* LBA search needs to know if same level or going for next appr level
SELECT SINGLE bukrs INTO lv_bukrs FROM /opt/vim_1head
SELECT SINGLE bukrs INTO lv_bukrs FROM /opt/vim_1head
* Coding flags for builidng Alternate next approvers
READ TABLE next_approvers INTO wa_next_approvers INDEX 1.
* For all the Users populate the coding flags
* check to ensure that invalid approver/empty approver is not populated
READ TABLE next_approvers INTO wa_next_approvers INDEX 1.
IF next_approvers IS INITIAL AND NOT ms_afs-fallback_user_fm IS INITIAL.
IF approval_complete NE 'X' AND next_approvers IS INITIAL.

PREVIOUS.item: * Get the Previous Approver for Level Based Approval depending on Customizing
* One Step: Always Level down, Path is always 1
* Back to Coder: Back to Level 1
* One Step: Descrease Path, if lower than 1, decrease the Level and get highest Path
* Back to Coder: Path = 1 and Level = 1
* One Step: Reject means that the complete Level will be canceled => decrease Level
* and get all Users for all Paths
* Back to Coder: Go back to starting Level 1, get all Paths for Level 1
* If Previous Approver is Initial, the Approval Process will be terminated
*-- Sequential or Parallel below Approver Level (treated as sequential)
*-- If Path <= 1 or in parallel, go level up and get highest path
* At some levels, all lines can be skip-approved, with path=0.
* We need to skip those lines by going into the yet lower levels.
*-- We are already in first level and first path => terminate approval Workflow
* Begin of VIMI-9566 - Check if initial Coder is identical to Current User => Terminate
*-- Get all Approver for determined path/level
WHEN '1'. "One Step Back => Means a complete Level
* At some levels, all lines can be skip-approved, with path=0.
* We need to skip those lines by going into the yet lower levels.
WHEN '2'. "Back to initial Coder => Start at first Level
*-- If level is already 1, terminate approval Workflow
* IF NOT lv_first_level IS INITIAL AND "VIMI-11916
*-- Get all Approver for coder/req Level for min path only
*-- Get all Approver for determined Level for all Paths
*-- Append found Users to exporting structure
IF sy-subrc = 0 AND NOT lt_stack_body IS INITIAL.
* Check if the previous user is valid or not
* Get the fallback user from fall back function module
* Configured in the AFS ID Configuration
